quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Balancing: Redox and Industrial Reactions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Balancing Complex Chemical Equations, Redox Reactions, Industrial Processes, Multiple Reactants and Products

questions.question:
___ $\text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7$ + ___ $\text{HCl}$ → ___ $\text{CrCl}_3$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ + ___ $\text{KCl}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7 + 14\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{CrCl}_3 + 3\text{Cl}_2 + 2\text{KCl} + 7\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is the redox reaction of potassium dichromate with hydrochloric acid.

questions.question:
___ $\text{MnO}_2$ + ___ $\text{HCl}$ → ___ $\text{MnCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{MnO}_2 + 4\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MnCl}_2 + \text{Cl}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. Manganese dioxide reacts with hydrochloric acid to produce chlorine gas.

questions.question:
___ $\text{FeCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ → ___ $\text{FeCl}_3$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{FeCl}_2 + \text{Cl}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{FeCl}_3$ is balanced. Iron(II) chloride is oxidized to iron(III) chloride by chlorine gas.

questions.question:
___ $\text{CuSO}_4$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ___ $\text{Cu}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ + ___ $\text{O}_2$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{CuSO}_4 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{Cu} + 2\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{O}_2$ is balanced. Left side: 2 Cu, 2 S, 8 O (from CuSO₄) + 2 O (from H₂O) = 10 O, 4 H; right side: 2 Cu, 2 S, 8 O (from H₂SO₄) + 2 O (from O₂) = 10 O, 4 H. This is the electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution.

questions.question:
___ $\text{NO}_2$ + ___ $\text{SO}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ___ $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ + ___ $\text{NO}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{NO}_2 + \text{SO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{NO}$ is balanced with coefficients 1, 1, 1, 1, 1. Left side: 1 N, 1 S, 5 O, 2 H; right side: 1 N, 1 S, 5 O, 2 H. This reaction represents the conversion of nitrogen dioxide and sulfur dioxide in the presence of water to form sulfuric acid and nitric oxide, relevant to atmospheric chemistry.

questions.question:
___ $\text{CaCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{CO}_2$ + ___ $\text{NH}_3$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ___ $\text{CaCO}_3$ + ___ $\text{NH}_4\text{Cl}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{CaCl}_2 + \text{CO}_2 + 2\text{NH}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CaCO}_3 + 2\text{NH}_4\text{Cl}$ is balanced. Left side: 1 Ca, 2 Cl, 1 C, 2 N, 6 H, 3 O (1 from CO₂ + 1 from H₂O); right side: 1 Ca, 1 C, 3 O (from CaCO₃), 2 N, 8 H, 2 Cl. Wait — let me verify carefully.

questions.question:
___ $\text{CaCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ → ___ $\text{CaCO}_3$ + ___ $\text{NaCl}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{CaCl}_2 + \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 \rightarrow \text{CaCO}_3 + 2\text{NaCl}$ is balanced with coefficients 1, 1, 1, 2. Left side: 1 Ca, 2 Cl, 2 Na, 1 C, 3 O; right side: 1 Ca, 1 C, 3 O, 2 Na, 2 Cl. This is a double replacement reaction that produces calcium carbonate (a precipitate) and sodium chloride.

questions.question:
___ $\text{FeCl}_3$ + ___ $\text{NaOH}$ → ___ $\text{Fe(OH)}_3$ + ___ $\text{NaCl}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{FeCl}_3 + 3\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Fe(OH)}_3 + 3\text{NaCl}$ is balanced with coefficients 1, 3, 1, 3. Left side: 1 Fe, 3 Cl, 3 Na, 3 O, 3 H; right side: 1 Fe, 3 O, 3 H, 3 Na, 3 Cl. This is a double replacement reaction forming iron(III) hydroxide precipitate and sodium chloride.

questions.question:
___ $\text{CaCl}_2$ + ___ $\text{Na}_3\text{PO}_4$ → ___ $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$ + ___ $\text{NaCl}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $3\text{CaCl}_2 + 2\text{Na}_3\text{PO}_4 \rightarrow \text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 6\text{NaCl}$ is balanced with coefficients 3, 2, 1, 6. Left side: 3 Ca, 6 Cl, 6 Na, 2 P, 8 O; right side: 3 Ca, 2 P, 8 O, 6 Na, 6 Cl. This is a double replacement reaction forming calcium phosphate (a precipitate) and sodium chloride.

questions.question:
___ $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8$ + ___ $\text{O}_2$ → ___ $\text{CO}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 3\text{CO}_2 + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced with coefficients 1, 5, 3, 4. Left side: 3 C, 8 H, 10 O; right side: 3 C, 8 H, 10 O (6 from CO₂ + 4 from H₂O). This is the complete combustion of propane.

questions.question:
___ $\text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7$ + ___ $\text{HCl}$ → ___ $\text{CrCl}_3$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$ + ___ $\text{KCl}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $\text{K}_2\text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7 + 14\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{CrCl}_3 + 3\text{Cl}_2 + 2\text{KCl} + 7\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is the redox reaction of potassium dichromate with hydrochloric acid.

questions.question:
___ $\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10}$ + ___ $\text{O}_2$ → ___ $\text{CO}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10} + 13\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 8\text{CO}_2 + 10\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced with coefficients 2, 13, 8, 10. Left side: 8 C, 20 H, 26 O; right side: 8 C, 20 H, 26 O (16 from CO₂ + 10 from H₂O). This is the complete combustion of butane.

questions.question:
___ $\text{CuFeS}_2$ + ___ $\text{O}_2$ → ___ $\text{Cu}_2\text{S}$ + ___ $\text{FeS}$ + ___ $\text{SO}_2$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $4\text{CuFeS}_2 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{Cu}_2\text{S} + 2\text{FeS} + 5\text{SO}_2$ is balanced. This is a simplified representation of copper pyrite smelting.

questions.question:
___ $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$ + ___ $\text{SiO}_2$ + ___ $\text{C}$ → ___ $\text{CaSiO}_3$ + ___ $\text{CO}$ + ___ $\text{P}_4$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 6\text{SiO}_2 + 10\text{C} \rightarrow 6\text{CaSiO}_3 + 10\text{CO} + \text{P}_4$ is balanced. This is the industrial production of phosphorus from phosphate rock.

questions.question:
___ $\text{NO}_2$ + ___ $\text{O}_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ + ___ $\text{SO}_2$ → ___ $\text{HNO}_3$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $4\text{NO}_2 + \text{O}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} + 2\text{SO}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{HNO}_3 + 2\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$ is balanced. This represents the formation of acid rain from nitrogen dioxide and sulfur dioxide.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered balancing even the most complex chemical equations! ⚖️🧪
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review redox reactions and industrial processes.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on balancing oxygen and hydrogen atoms last.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the Law of Conservation of Mass and practice balancing simpler equations first.
